name: Dr David Folch
role: Chair, Department of Geography, Planning and Recreation
focus: Geocomputation • Urban demographic change • Spatial data methods
bio: Dr. David Folch is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Geography, Planning and Recreation at Northern Arizona University. His research focuses on the data, measures, and algorithms that support empirical research and public policy decisions. He uses geocomputational approaches to study issues of demographic change in U.S. cities and neighborhoods, with work spanning the measurement of residential segregation and social vulnerability and the development of spatial approaches for reducing uncertainty in American Community Survey estimates. He holds degrees in Geography and Economics and serves as an associate editor of the journal Papers in Regional Science.
